page: Amazon Web Services i7i.xlarge is a storage-optimized virtual server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 2.4 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 320 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 937 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a strong contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The server achieves top-tier status in Geekbench single-core and text-processing benchmarks, alongside strong single-threaded performance in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core and parallelized workloads show limitations, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and software compilation with clang falling into the bottom 25% tier. Memory read bandwidth is also relatively weak, though write bandwidth for large blocks and database operations remain in the average tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the i7i.xlarge offers high storage density and modern DDR5 memory, making it a specialized option for workloads that require fast local storage and strong single-threaded performance rather than massive parallel compute. It is well-suited for relational databases, NoSQL stores, caching layers like Redis, and single-threaded text or data processing tasks.
description: The Amazon Web Services i7i.xlarge is a storage-optimized virtual server running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 4 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor at 2.4 GHz, 32.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 937 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Architecturally, it supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. Performance benchmarks show a distinct tradeoff: the instance delivers top-tier single-core and text-processing capabilities, alongside strong single-threaded compression speeds. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and memory read bandwidth fall into the lower tiers. With a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps and no integrated GPU, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-heavy workloads requiring fast local disk access. It is best suited for database operations, Redis caching, and single-threaded data processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and 937 GB NVMe SSD. It offers top-tier single-core performance but exhibits weaker multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and 937 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A storage-optimized instance pairing fast local NVMe SSDs and DDR5 memory with strong single-core performance for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory
937 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Intel Xeon 8559C processor with AVX-512 and AMX support
Top-tier single-core and text-processing benchmark results
Weaker multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth
Optimized for databases, Redis caching, and single-threaded processing
